column1: # framing_invariants.tsv -- the mechanized memory of cross-section soft-claim consistency.
# WHY: the squeeze's machine gates verify EVIDENCE-BOUND claims (CITE/RESULT). They do not
# verify mutual consistency of INTERPRETIVE / framing claims across sections -- a soft-truth
# vs soft-truth comparison with no executable lower bound (see paper-impl.md, "completing the
# gates"). That residual is caught only by a disjoint editorial pass (Gate A). This ledger
# makes the residual SHRINK monotonically: every coherent-and-wrong framing the editorial
# pass catches is recorded here as a hard check, so it can never silently return.
# It does NOT (cannot) catch a brand-new, unregistered framing defect -- coverage is the
# ceiling. That is why editorial_gate.py keeps a disjoint Gate A as a STANDING gate.
# Row kinds (tab-separated), checked against tex/paper.tex (LaTeX commands stripped,
# whitespace collapsed, case-insensitive):
#   BANNED <regex> <rationale>          -- a previously-caught coherent-and-wrong phrasing;
#                                          FAIL if it reappears anywhere.
#   MAX    <regex> <n> <rationale>      -- this pattern may match at most n times; FAIL if
#                                          exceeded (superlative / ranking uniqueness).
# To register a NEW invariant after an editorial catch: add the corrected canonical position
# to the rationale and the stale phrasing as BANNED (or a uniqueness cap as MAX).
BANNED
column3: circle 119: in-house evidence is TWO complementary strands (live instance-B result + reflexive n=1); calling the reflexive case the single "strongest" contradicts the abstract. Caught by the reflexive editorial pass.

column1: BANNED
column2: one piece of efficacy evidence we do have today is reflexive
column3: circle 119: false post-R2/R3; the live instance-B underspecification/cross-model result is also efficacy evidence. Corrected to "two kinds: a live one and a reflexive one".

column1: BANNED
column2: closest in-house evidence that the disjoint gate catches coherent-and-wrong is reflexive
column3: circle 118: superseded by the two-strand framing; the live result is the more direct validation.

column1: BANNED
column3: R6 (circle 113): the pipeline comparison was EXECUTED as an exploratory study; "piloted but deferred" understates it. Powered/multi-tier version is what remains future work.

column1: BANNED
column3: circle 118: the cross-model cast was RUN and measured (R3); framing it as a planned ablation undersells a result we hold.

column1: BANNED
column3: review5 #3 rerank: the reflexive n=1 self-application is an ILLUSTRATION, not the strongest evidence; the two live findings (external UEFI defects + instance-B underspecification) are. Ranking the reflexive study top re-creates the circle-118/119 coherent-and-wrong.

column1: MAX
column4: review5 #3 (deliberate rerank, replacing the old "two coordinate strands, neither ranked"): the framing now has an evidence HIERARCHY -- the two LIVE findings (external UEFI defects + the live-model instance-B underspecification) are the strongest/load-bearing evidence, above the seeded consistency-check instances and the reflexive n=1. Exactly two superlatives license that ranking (abstract + contribution iii); a THIRD needs review, and ranking the reflexive study top is BANNED above.

column1: MAX
column4: circle 119: the disjoint Gate A caught an evidence-ranking superlative in the OTHER word order ("evidence ... strongest on real, non-seeded") that the first MAX rule missed -- coverage is a floor, not a ceiling. This row mechanizes that catch so the evidence-before-superlative order cannot return either.
